title: asplenium trichomanes var. vespertinum
intro: Asplenium trichomanes var. vespertinum, also known as the Vesper-rustyback, is a variety of the Maidenhair spleenwort plant, which is a type of fern native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Asplenium trichomanes var. vespertinum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote the removal of mucus from the lungs, making it a valuable herb for those suffering from respiratory problems. Asplenium trichomanes var. vespertinum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried leaves and stems being used for herbal remedies.
